dimensions.meaning: Confidence that the artifact identity and version are correctly resolved.
Confidence that the represented claim accurately reflects the cited passage.
Degree to which the method is specified sufficiently for technical evaluation.
Adequacy of sample size, coverage, representativeness, labels, and exclusions.
Adequacy of statistical design, uncertainty reporting, controls, and multiplicity treatment.
Strength of evaluation outside the development data, site, instrument, or cohort.
Strength of independent confirmation by parties not responsible for the original work.
Availability and sufficiency of code, data, parameters, protocols, and execution details.
Risk that financial, organizational, or authorship interests affect interpretation.
Risk arising from corrections, expressions of concern, retractions, or material updates.
Applicability of the evidence to the relevant TrueColor capability and operating conditions.
